project.objective: Who were the parents of Christiana Clark, born 29 January 1783 in Pennsylvania, who married William Clark, lived in Coshocton County, Ohio, and died 27 December 1848 in Monroe, Green County, Wisconsin?

questions.question: What do Green County, Wisconsin death, probate, and newspaper records (1848–1855) say about Christiana Clark's parents or family origins?
questions.rationale: Wisconsin state death registration did not begin until 1852, making a civil death certificate unavailable for Christiana's 27 December 1848 death. However, Green County probate records, newspaper death notices in the Monroe Atlas or similar, and early Green County histories are the records closest in time to her death and most likely to name her parents or state her origin. This is the gatekeeper question — it anchors her final context and may yield a direct statement of parentage before we work backward through Ohio and Pennsylvania.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: FamilySearch-accessible Green County Wisconsin records exhausted: death index nil (civil registration began 1852, predating Christiana's Dec 1848 death), probate packet full-text search non-functional for the Wisconsin Probate Estate Files image collection, 1850 and 1855 censuses searched, land records searched. Two interactive-capture searches remain outstanding — Newspapers.com (obituary search for Christiana Clark Dec 1848–early 1849) and FindAGrave/Wisconsin gravestone projects — deferred because they require authenticated browser capture unavailable in an autonomous run. No direct statement of Christiana Clark's parentage was found in any consulted record. FAN leads identified: Joseph Clark (b. PA ~1780, Decatur Green Co. WI, possible sibling) and Daniel W Clark (b. PA ~1805, Monroe Green Co. WI, possible son not yet in tree). Research path shifts to Coshocton County Ohio and Berks County Pennsylvania records, where the preponderance of direct evidence is expected to reside.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: No direct parentage evidence found. The consulted Wisconsin records yield no statement naming Christiana Clark's parents. Two FAN leads (Joseph Clark, Daniel W Clark) are promising but speculative — neither names a parent.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch Wisconsin records searched across five record types (death index, probate FTS, two censuses, land deeds). Newspapers.com and FindAGrave deferred to an interactive session. GenealogyBank Wisconsin newspapers not searched (autonomous mode; URL documented in log_012).
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Census results drawn from FamilySearch indexed personas; original images for the two FAN-lead households (ark:/61903/1:1:M4DV-MMT, ark:/61903/1:1:M4DN-TWB) not independently examined from the NARA microfilm. Noted as a gap in src_002.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Not applicable — no parentage conclusion reached. Identified FAN leads rest on a single source each (one 1850 census record per household); corroboration is the purpose of the next research phase.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Not applicable — no direct or indirect evidence of parentage found in the records consulted.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicting parentage claims identified in the Wisconsin-scope research. The absence of evidence is not a conflict.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Moderate. A newspaper obituary for Christiana Clark (d. 27 Dec 1848, Monroe WI) in a contemporary Green County paper could directly name her parents or Pennsylvania origin county. A Find A Grave memorial with a gravestone inscription could supply the same. These sources are deferred, not exhausted; overturn risk from them is real but estimated low given the sparseness of 1848 frontier Wisconsin newspapers.

questions.question: What does the baptismal entry for Christiana Clark (baptized 2 March 1783, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania) name as her father and mother?
questions.rationale: Christiana Clark's baptism on 2 March 1783 at Rehrersburg in Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania is already documented in the tree. German-American church baptism registers in 18th-century Berks County almost universally name both parents — the father's full name and the mother's maiden name — making this the single highest-yield record for identifying Christiana's parents. The baptism entry, if found, carries primary information: the parents themselves provided the names to the pastor at the time of the rite. Tulpehocken Township was settled by German Reformed and Lutheran immigrants; both congregations kept detailed registers. FamilySearch has indexed many Berks County German church records, and the Historical Society of Berks County holds originals. The existing tree entry for this baptism cites a date and place but does not yet name the parents — the source behind that entry must be examined, and the original church register consulted if the current source is only an index or compiled genealogy. This question directly advances the project objective and, once answered, unblocks all further research into the Clark (or likely German-surname) family of Tulpehocken Township.

items.rationale: Wisconsin Death Index 1820–1907 (collection 1946789 / 1940759) is indexed and searchable. A death index entry for Christiana Clark (d. 27 Dec 1848) may point to an original registration or give informant data. Quick indexed check before browsing unindexed probate images.

Wisconsin Probate Estate Files 1848–1948 (collection 1874190) is a browse-only image collection of 1.4M images. Green County probate packets appear as individual full-text-searchable image groups (imageGroupPrefix 004659615). An estate file for Christiana Clark or William Clark would name heirs, debts, and creditors — potentially naming Christiana's siblings or her parents' estate as a source of assets. Search the full-text probate packets by 'Clark' for Green County ca. 1848–1855.

Christiana died 27 December 1848; the 1850 federal census was taken in mid-1850. William Clark, widower, and any children still in the household would appear. The household composition may indicate which children remained, their approximate birth years and birthplaces, and may prompt a search for surviving children's death records that could name Christiana's parents. Also checks whether any Clark relatives live nearby (FAN cluster).

Wisconsin State Census 1855 (collection 1443825) is indexed. William Clark's household five years after Christiana's death may reveal additional family members, birthplaces of older children (a Pennsylvania-born child could corroborate the family's origin), and neighbors who may be Clark relatives or associates.

GenealogyBank holds Wisconsin newspaper archives 1833–1992 (genealogybank.com/explore/newspapers/all/usa/wisconsin). Monroe, Wisconsin had newspapers by the late 1840s. A death notice or obituary for Christiana Clark in December 1848 or January 1849 is the single record most likely to name her parents or state her Pennsylvania origin explicitly. Also search USGenWeb Wisconsin obituary project (usgwarchives.net/obits/wi/obitswi.htm).

Green County deed records 1836–1857 are available as full-text-searchable image groups on FamilySearch (imageGroupPrefix 005713754, 008550112). Land transactions by William Clark and any Clark-surname co-grantors or witnesses may identify brothers or other relatives of Christiana. Deed grantors/grantees from Pennsylvania sometimes transferred land with family members, and a deed signed by a 'brother' or 'heir' of Christiana could point directly to her parentage.

Wisconsin Historical Society (wisconsinhistory.org) holds local county histories and biographical compilations for Green County. An 1881 or 1884 county history sketch for William Clark or any of their children may state Christiana's parentage explicitly. Also check the Wisconsin Gravestone Photo Project (wisconsingravestones.org) and FindAGrave for Christiana's burial marker in Monroe — a gravestone inscription sometimes names parents or gives exact birth details.

Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950 (collection 1681005, 2.5M records indexed) is the fastest path. A search for Christina or Christiana Clark born ~March 1783 in Berks County / Tulpehocken Township may return an indexed entry naming her parents. German-American church baptism entries routinely record both parents' names. If the Altamaha Lutheran Church (Rehrersburg) submitted records to this collection, the parents will appear here without needing to browse the unindexed original images.

Full-text search in the Tulpehocken Township German Reformed / Host Church multi-volume compilation (imageGroupNumber 007899351_001_M9MP-SN8 and siblings 007899351_002, 007899351_003, 007899351_005 — fulltextSearchable: true). These volumes cover baptisms 1730-1896 in Tulpehocken and adjoining areas. A full-text co-occurrence search for 'Clark' within these volumes may surface a christening entry naming a Clark child and parents. Use search-full-text skill with imageGroupNumber and surname filter.

items.jurisdiction: Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania
items.rationale: Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church, Rehrersburg, Berks PA — image group 007767897_013_M94S-YWF — 150 images, births/christenings 1757-1842. This is the specific congregation at Rehrersburg where Christiana Clark was baptized on 2 March 1783. The volume is NOT indexed and NOT full-text searchable; it must be browsed image by image via the image-reader skill. Target: locate the page for late February or early March 1783 and read the entry, which should name both parents (father and mother's maiden name). This is the highest-evidential-value item: an original church register is a primary source, and the parents supplied the information at the time of the rite.

Dedicated search for the marriage record of Christiana Clark's parents — required for any parentage question (record-type-guide). Once the baptism entry (items 1-3) names the parents, a marriage record for that couple in Berks County 1760-1782 (before Christiana's birth) would: (a) confirm the couple as a unit; (b) supply the mother's maiden name if not given in the baptism entry; and (c) by its date relative to Christiana's 1783 birth, corroborate the parentage conclusion. Search Pennsylvania, Church Marriages, 1682-1976 (collection 2466360) and Pennsylvania, County Marriages, 1775-1991 (collection 1589502) in the Berks County jurisdiction.

The 1790 U.S. Federal Census (FamilySearch indexed) lists only heads of household but places a Clark family in Tulpehocken Township or adjacent Berks County areas if Christiana's father was alive in 1790. A Clark head in his 40s-60s in Berks County (consistent with having a child born 1783) corroborates the family's location and composition. The census can also show if Joseph Clark (I1, b. PA ~1780) and other potential siblings appear in the same dwelling, supporting or refuting the sibling hypothesis.

FAN pivot: Joseph Clark (tree person I1, b. PA ~1780, Decatur Green County WI 1850) is 3 years older than Christiana and shares her Pennsylvania birthplace and Wisconsin destination — a plausible sibling. If he was baptized in the same Rehrersburg congregation ~1778-1780, his baptism entry would name their common parents. Search Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950 (collection 1681005) for a Joseph Clark born ~1778-1780 in Berks County / Tulpehocken. A matching entry naming the same parents as Christiana's baptism would provide corroborating, independent evidence of parentage.

Ancestry, Pennsylvania, Church and Town Records, 1708-1985 (collection 2451) is an independent collection of Pennsylvania church registers including many Berks County German Reformed and Lutheran congregations not fully covered by FamilySearch. If the Altamaha Lutheran Church or a neighboring Rehrersburg congregation submitted records to this Ancestry collection, a search for Christina Clark born 1783 in Berks County may surface the baptism entry with parental names. Fallback for items 1-3 when FamilySearch sources are exhausted.

Image group 007856339_010 — Zion (Spiess) Church, Stony Creek Mills, Berks County PA; combined Reformed and Lutheran records including records of Pastor John William Boos (who may have served multiple congregations on a circuit including Tulpehocken Township). Looking for Clark baptism ca. 1783.

Image group 007856339_011 — Pastor's record, Reformed churches in Berks County, 1777-1810. A circuit Reformed pastor's record covers multiple congregations across the county; most likely to contain a Clark baptism from Tulpehocken/Bethel Township area ca. 1783.

Image group 004022578_005 — Host Reformed Church, Heidelberg Township, Berks County. Heidelberg Township borders Tulpehocken Township at Rehrersburg; Host Reformed is the German Reformed congregation serving the same Rehrersburg-area community as Altamaha Lutheran. High-priority source for the Clark 1783 baptism.

log.notes: No results for Christiana Clark in Wisconsin Death Index 1820-1907 (collection 1940759). Consistent with Wisconsin civil registration beginning 1852; pre-1852 deaths may be absent or sparse in this collection.

Variant spelling Christina Clark also returns nil in Wisconsin Death Index. Collection appears to lack coverage for Dec 1848 death. pli_001 exhausted at FamilySearch — no death index entry exists for Christiana/Christina Clark d. 1848 Green County WI.

16 results for Clark households in Green County WI 1850 census. Notable households in Monroe, Green Co.: (1) Daniel W Clark (born PA 1805) — head, household ark:/61903/1:2:MWXN-FZ2; (2) Joseph Clark — head, household ark:/61903/1:2:MWXN-FH5; (3) Salas Clark — head, household ark:/61903/1:2:MWXN-FCM (children born IN). William Clark (Christiana's husband) not found as head of household; he may have died Dec 1848–mid 1850. Daniel W Clark (born PA 1805) is potentially Christiana's son — reading full households next.

query.note: broadened from Green County — no results found in Green County in prior search

log.notes: 46 William/Wm Clark households found across Wisconsin in 1855 state census, but none in Green County. Also searched specifically with residencePlace Green, Wisconsin: 3 results returned, none in Green County (in Adams and Polk counties). William Clark (Christiana's husband) absent from 1855 Wisconsin census entirely in Green County. He likely died between Dec 1848 and 1855.

query.note: ad-hoc follow-up to identify Joseph Clark and siblings in Green County 1850

log.notes: Joseph Clark (born OH 1816) confirmed in Monroe, Green County WI 1850 (ark:/61903/1:1:M4DJ-878) — consistent with tree person LZLH-PPW. Key find: separate Joseph Clark (born PA 1780) in Decatur, Green County WI (ark:/61903/1:1:M4DV-MMT) — born same state as Christiana (PA), approx. 3 years older, possibly her brother. Also: Alexander Clark household in Decatur Green County (needs reading).
Fulltext search for Christiana Clark in Wisconsin 1845-1860 returned nil. Year/place filters may be excluding probate packets that lack metadata dates.
Fulltext search for William Clark in Green County Wisconsin probate 1848-1855 returned nil. Year/place/type filters may be too restrictive for image-only probate packets.

query.note: These are the earliest-numbered Green County probate packets in the 004659615 series (cases 222-226)

log.notes: FTS returns 0 results on individual probate packet image groups despite them being marked fulltextSearchable:true in volume_search. This is a systematic failure of the FTS index for these packets — tried 4 individual volumes, all return 0 for '+Clark'. Also tried Wisconsin probate estate packets with broader place/type filters in earlier session (log_006, log_007): also 0 results. The wills/deeds collection (008198364_003_M99D-F1K) showed a 'William Clark' but dated 1897 — not our subject. FTS search of Green County probate packets is effectively non-functional at FamilySearch. Recommend Ancestry.com as fallback for Wisconsin probate estate files (collection 8815459 on Ancestry)."

Two deed records found naming William Clark in Monroe, Green County: (1) May 20, 1846: Joseph Kelly and Malinda Kelly (Town of Monroe) sold Lots 2 and 4, Block 28, Wattars Addition to Monroe to William Clark for $20 — recorded Green County, Wisconsin Territory, witnesses Wm Rittenhouse and Joseph Forney, notarized by Wm Rittenhouse. No state of origin for William Clark stated in this deed. (2) January 6, 1847: William Clark and 'Hester his wife' of Green County (AI possibly misread county as 'Sion') sold Lots 6 and 8, Block 28, William's Addition to Monroe to John Christy of County of Coshocton, State of Ohio for $300, with acknowledgment January 8, 1847. SIGNIFICANT: Grantee John Christy is identified as being from Coshocton County, Ohio — exactly where our Christiana Clark lived before Wisconsin. Wife's name transcribed as 'Hester' by AI image reader — this may be a misread of 'Christiana' in cursive script, or there are two William Clarks in Monroe. Neither deed states William Clark's state of origin or Christiana's maiden name. Also found Daniel W Clark mentioned in a 1847 deed (ark:/61903/3:1:3Q9M-C3Q8-9STZ-V) as 'Daniel, W. Clark' with places including Ohio."
Curated Newspapers.com link for Wisconsin 1840-1860: Wisconsin Historical Newspapers at newspapers.com/place-wisconsin/?xid=186. GenealogyBank not in curated results; will use direct search URL.

Curated FindAGrave links for Wisconsin — general Wisconsin cemetery browse and named Milwaukee/Madison cemeteries. No specific Monroe, Green County cemetery links. Will use memorial search URL for Christiana Clark directly.

DEFERRED — requires interactive user capture and is not obtainable in an autonomous run. Generated URL: https://www.newspapers.com/search/?query=Christiana+Clark&dr_year=1848-1850&dr_place=Monroe%2C+Wisconsin — searches Wisconsin newspapers 1848-1850 for Christiana Clark death notice or obituary. The Monroe Atlas (or similar early Green County newspaper) would be the primary target. Note: Wisconsin had limited newspaper coverage in 1848; a death notice for a December 1848 death may not appear in surviving digitized issues. In an interactive session, also search GenealogyBank at https://www.genealogybank.com/explore/newspapers/all/usa/wisconsin?date-range-start=1848-01-01&date-range-end=1850-12-31 and USGenWeb Wisconsin obituary project at https://usgwarchives.net/obits/wi/obitswi.htm"

DEFERRED — requires interactive user capture and is not obtainable in an autonomous run. Generated URL: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/search?firstname=Christiana&lastname=Clark&birthyear=1783&deathyear=1848&location=Monroe%2C+Green+County%2C+Wisconsin — searches for Christiana Clark (born 1783 PA, died 1848 Monroe WI) burial memorial. A gravestone inscription is the single record most likely to name her parents or give exact birth details per plan rationale. Also search Wisconsin Gravestone Photo Project at https://www.wisconsingravestones.org — search Green County for Clark surname. Wisconsin Historical Society county history: https://www.wisconsinhistory.org/Records/Article/CS3835 — check 1881 History of Green County for William Clark or Christiana Clark biographical sketches. Variant spelling 'Christina Clark' should also be tried on FindAGrave in an interactive session."

Single result returned (Damson Clark, male) — not the subject. No indexed baptism entry for Christiana/Christina Clark born 1782-1784 in Pennsylvania in this collection. Confirms the Altamaha Lutheran register (pli_010) is not indexed in this collection and requires direct image browse.

query.keywords: +Clark +Christina or +Clark; imageGroupNumbers tried: 007899351_001, 007899351_002, 007899351_003; also tried +Clark +Rehrersburg unscoped; +Klar +Christina PA 1778-1788

log.notes: Tulpehocken German Reformed volumes 007899351_001/002/003 return zero results for all Clark searches, including unfiltered — these image groups lack AI transcriptions in the FTS system. Broader unscoped searches for +Clark +Rehrersburg and +Clark +Tulpehocken returned 20+ results but all from late 19th-20th century Pennsylvania biography/history collections, not church registers. No Christina Clark baptism c. 1783 found via FTS. Proceeding to direct image browse of Altamaha Lutheran register (pli_010)."

Three Clark heads of household found in Berks County 1790: (1) 'Chrisr Clark', Bethel Township — MOST SIGNIFICANT: Bethel Twp borders Tulpehocken Twp where Christiana was baptized at Rehrersburg; 'Chrisr' likely abbreviates 'Christian', consistent with German-American naming. ARK ark:/61903/1:1:XHKJ-SS3. (2) 'Thos Clark', Caernarvon Township (ARK ark:/61903/1:1:XHKJ-98R) — further south, less likely. (3) 'Robert Clark', Amity Township (ARK ark:/61903/1:1:XHKN-YYN) — southwest Berks, not near Tulpehocken. The 1790 census only names the head; household tally numbers require original image (image-reader currently unavailable)."

query.purpose: Locate records linked to Christiana Clark FamilySearch person GP3R-215

log.notes: Highly significant findings: (1) Green Co. WI Probate Estate File 1852 (ark:/61903/3:1:939J-HT78-7) names BOTH 'Christiana Clark' AND 'Joseph Clarke' in Monroe, Green Co. WI — Joseph's presence in Christiana's estate proceedings is strong evidence of a sibling/heir relationship. (2) Green Co. WI Probate 1851 (ark:/61903/3:1:939J-HT7V-5) names 'State of Christina Clark' (= her estate) and Adam Shrake. (3) Green Co. WI Probate 1849 (ark:/61903/3:1:939J-HT7G-X) names 'Christina Clark'. (4) Green Co. WI Deed 1850-1853 (ark:/61903/3:1:3Q9M-C3Q8-9T84) names both Christiana Clark and Joseph Clark/Clarke in Town of Monroe. (5) Coshocton OH Deed 1840 names Shrake family. Adam Shrake appears prominently as administrator/creditor in Christiana's estate. Joseph Clark/Clarke (b. PA ~1780) is 3 years OLDER than Christiana (b. 1783) so cannot be her son — must be sibling or collateral relative."

query.description: Clark marriage in Berks County PA German Lutheran church records 1760-1782

log.notes: Searched for Clark marriage records in Berks County PA 1760-1782. Retrieved 1,567 results, but all examined records were from England, Delaware, or Philadelphia — none from Berks County German Lutheran churches. The indexed PA church marriage records for rural Berks County German-speaking congregations (including Altamaha/Rehrersburg area) are not available in the FamilySearch indexed collections for this period; they exist only as browse-only image groups. The parents' marriage record for Christiana Clark's parents cannot be found by indexed search. Negative result is inconclusive — the record may exist in unindexed German Reformed or Lutheran registers covering Tulpehocken Township/Bethel Township area but is not accessible via indexed search.

query.description: Joseph Clark baptism in Pennsylvania Births and Christenings collection 1681005, Berks County, 1777-1783

log.notes: Searched collection 1681005 (Pennsylvania Births and Christenings 1709-1950) for Joseph Clark in Berks County 1777-1783. Two 'Entry FOR Joseph Clark' records were read but proved incorrect: one from Philadelphia (1790) and one from Kittanning (1849). No Joseph Clark baptism from Berks County in this period found in the indexed collection. The Altamaha Lutheran Church register for Rehrersburg/Tulpehocken Township is NOT indexed in collection 1681005 — it exists only as browse-only image group 007767897. Any Joseph Clark baptism there would be invisible to indexed search. Negative result is inconclusive — the sibling relationship between Joseph and Christiana Clark remains supported by the Wisconsin probate and deed records but cannot yet be confirmed via a shared Berks County baptism record through indexed search.

query.description: Browse Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church Rehrersburg Berks County PA baptism index 1757-1842 for Clark surname entries
query.lookingFor: Clark baptism entries, specifically Christina/Christiana Clark born ~1783 with parents named

log.notes: Browsed 4 images from the Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church baptism index (image group 007767897). Image 00859 (page 31) failed to load (fetch failed — intermittent connectivity). Pages 30, 32, 33, and 34 were successfully read. The CLARK section appears on page 34 (image 00862) and contains EXACTLY ONE ENTRY: Anna Maria Clarck, born 27 Nov 1798, christened 9 Dec 1798, daughter of CHRISTOPH CLARCK AND BARBARA (serial number 01130-5). There is NO entry for a Christina or Christiana Clark born around January 1783 in the Altamaha Lutheran register. The 1783 baptism attributed to Christiana Clark is not in this church's records. The Christoph Clarck / Barbara family is significant: Christoph Clark ('Chrisr Clark') appears in the adjacent Bethel Township, Berks County in the 1790 census, and Christoph having a daughter at this church in 1798 corroborates his longstanding presence in the Rehrersburg area. Christiana Clark's 1783 baptism must be sought in the German Reformed church records for Tulpehocken Township / Rehrersburg area (Zion's Reformed or similar), or possibly a different Lutheran congregation not represented in this index.

query.description: FTS search for Clark/Clarck + Christiana in PA church records 1778-1788

log.notes: Ran two FTS searches for Clark baptism in Pennsylvania 1778-1788: (1) '+Clarck +Christiana' PA, (2) '+Clark +Christiana' PA. Both returned 0 results. Also ran '+Clark +Christina' in Berks County PA 1778-1788 — 0 results. The German Reformed church records for Berks County / Tulpehocken Township are not indexed in the FamilySearch FTS system for this period. These records exist only as browse-only image groups not AI-transcribed for FTS. Pivoting to volume_search to find the German Reformed church register for Rehrersburg/Tulpehocken area.

query.description: Ancestry fallback search for Clark baptism, Berks County PA 1783
query.rationale: Fallback for pli_010 (Lutheran baptism index). However, pli_010 image browse confirmed the Altamaha Lutheran register contains NO Clark baptism for 1783 — only one Clark entry (Anna Maria Clarck, 1798, parents Christoph Clarck and Barbara). Ancestry holds the same Lutheran records digitized on FamilySearch; an Ancestry search for the same Lutheran register would yield the same negative result. Deferred in autonomous mode — no user to capture PDF — and superseded in priority by German Reformed church records for Berks County, which are the correct next target.

log.notes: Deferred — autonomous mode, no interactive user to capture. Additionally, this fallback is now moot: pli_010 image browse confirmed no 1783 Clark baptism in the Altamaha Lutheran register; looking for the same Lutheran record on Ancestry would yield nothing. The German Reformed circuit pastor's records for Berks County (image groups 007856339_010 and 007856339_011) are the correct next source.

query.description: FTS search for Clark in Tulpehocken Host St. Johns Reformed Church Births/Baptisms Vol. 2

log.notes: FTS search within image group 004022578_005 (Tulpehocken Host St. John's Reformed Church, Births/Baptisms Vol. 2) returned 3 hits for 'Clark': (1) ARK 3:1:3QSQ-G92C-6WYJ, dated 1781, includes 'Jonas Clark Jr' in garbled OCR names list — likely Joseph Clark b. ~1780, the FAN sibling candidate for Christiana; (2) ARK 3:1:3QSQ-G92C-6WYD, dated 1847, includes 'Anne Maria Clark'; (3) ARK 3:1:3QSQ-G92C-6WTX, dated 1848, includes 'Clark Daniel Desmond'. The 1781 Clark record is the most significant — if 'Jonas Clark Jr' is actually Joseph Clark son of [Christoph] Clark, it would place the Clark family at this congregation 2 years before Christiana's 1783 baptism. NOTE: All names in FTS results are heavily garbled OCR from old German Kurrent script. The actual register text must be read from the original images.

assertions.informant_bias_notes: Birth year calculated from reported age on 1850 census; unknown household member answered for the dwelling. Age reporting on pre-1880 censuses is frequently approximate.
Birthplace stated in the record; unknown household member answered for the dwelling. Aligns with Christiana Clark's known Pennsylvania birthplace — potential sibling or close relative context.
Enumerator visited the dwelling and recorded the household at this location.

No relationship column in 1850 census. Elizabeth Clark is the only adult female in the household and is listed immediately after the head; spousal relationship inferred from household position and naming pattern. The 41-year age gap (Joseph ~70, Elizabeth ~29) is notable — she may be a second wife, a daughter, or another relative. This inference requires corroboration.

person_evidence.rationale: 1850 U.S. Census, Decatur, Green County, WI: Joseph Clark listed as head of household. Name matches persona p_180306026 materialized as I1. Single-source stub; confidence probable pending corroboration.
Birth year ~1780 (age 70) from 1850 census for Joseph Clark (I1). Consistent with being approx. 3 years older than Christiana Clark (b. 1783), supporting possible sibling hypothesis.
Birthplace Pennsylvania stated for Joseph Clark (I1) in 1850 census. Matches Christiana Clark's known Pennsylvania birthplace — key FAN indicator.
Residence Decatur, Green County, Wisconsin 1850 for Joseph Clark (I1); enumerator-stated primary information.

Spousal relationship to Elizabeth Clark inferred from household position. 41-year age gap (Joseph ~70, Elizabeth ~29) makes wife inference speculative — Elizabeth may be a daughter or other relative rather than a spouse.
Assertion a_005 also bears on Elizabeth Clark (I3) as the person inferred as Joseph's spouse. Same 41-year age gap caveat; confidence speculative.
